expectations.text: Identifies this as a deemed export situation under 22 CFR 120.50
expectations.evidence: Executive Summary calls out 'deemed export rule' and references 22 C.F.R. Parts 120-130; Step 1 discusses deemed export, though the specific § 120.50 citation is not prominent — it appears in the references table as '22 C.F.R. Part 120: ITAR General Provisions'.

expectations.text: States that a TAA or individual licence may be required before access is granted
expectations.evidence: Step 4 covers DSP-5 and TAA (22 C.F.R. § 124.1) as authorization options required before the engineer arrives.

expectations.text: Explains Technology Control Plan (TCP) requirements for foreign visitor access
expectations.evidence: Step 5d is titled 'Implement an Access Control Plan' covering physical and IT access controls; TCP is mentioned in Step 6 medium-term remediation section.

expectations.text: Mentions screening against DDTC debarred parties list and OFAC SDN list
expectations.evidence: Step 1 lists 'DDTC Debarred Parties List (22 C.F.R. Part 127)' and 'OFAC SDN and Consolidated Sanctions List' as required screening.

expectations.text: Clarifies that German citizenship (not work visa status) determines export destination for deemed export purposes
expectations.evidence: Step 1 states 'Confirm citizenship: A German citizen is a national of Germany' and notes 'Temporary visa holders are foreign nationals' while permanent residents are US persons — the citizenship vs. visa distinction is made, though less explicitly than ideal.
